title: QBI Deduction Optimization Through Phase-Out Planning

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must strategically manage taxable income and business structure when income falls within QBI phase-out ranges to maximize Section 199A deduction through timing income, increasing deductions, and optimizing W-2 wages
criteria: Taxable income near or within phase-out range requiring strategic planning to optimize deduction
Strategies to reduce taxable income below thresholds: retirement contributions, charitable donations, equipment purchases
For non-SSTB businesses: Increase W-2 wages to maximize deduction within wage/property limitations
For SSTB businesses: Critical to keep income below phase-out range or completely above to avoid partial limitation
Consider income and deduction timing strategies: defer income to next year, accelerate deductions to current year
Analyze S Corporation vs sole proprietorship: S Corp wages reduce QBI but satisfy W-2 wage limitation
Multi-year planning to smooth income and avoid bouncing in and out of phase-out ranges

qbi_optimization_strategies.threshold_management: Keep income below $197,300/$394,600 for full deduction without limitations
qbi_optimization_strategies.phase_out_planning: Within phase-out range, strategic planning can preserve significant deduction
qbi_optimization_strategies.w2_wage_strategy: Increase W-2 wages through S Corp election or employee hiring to maximize non-SSTB deduction
qbi_optimization_strategies.equipment_purchases: Qualified property increases UBIA providing alternative limitation calculation (25% wages + 2.5% UBIA)
qbi_optimization_strategies.retirement_contributions: Maximize retirement plan contributions to reduce taxable income below thresholds
qbi_optimization_strategies.sstb_critical_planning: SSTB owners must prioritize staying below phase-out range; lose entire deduction above $247,300/$494,600

restrictions: SSTB phase-out eliminates deduction entirely; cannot optimize through W-2 wages or property
Reasonable compensation requirements limit ability to manipulate S Corporation wage levels
Equipment purchase timing must consider bonus depreciation phase-out reducing immediate deduction benefit
Retirement contribution limits cap ability to reduce taxable income through this strategy
Multi-year income smoothing may not be feasible for businesses with volatile or unpredictable income
State tax implications of optimization strategies may differ from federal creating additional complexity
Trading off current year income for deduction may have long-term implications for business growth
Phase-out cliff effects at upper threshold ($247,300/$494,600) create all-or-nothing scenarios for SSTB
